data.title: Walmart Stock (WMT) Wilts Despite Analysts Hailing Sparky Chatbot Sales Boom
data.description:
Shares in retail giant Walmart (WMT) were lower today despite analysts stressing that the group was still in good shape to grab market share and keep making "Sparky" AI chatbot gains in the year ahead.

Upbeat Walmart Analysts

data.author: David Craik

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Struck Down by Supreme Court in 6-3 Ruling
data.description:
The Supreme Court announced Friday that it had struck down President Trump's tariffs in a 6‑3 ruling. SCOTUS said Trump exceeded his executive power when invoking the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to enforce sweeping "reciprocal" tariffs. The act allows the president to impose economic sanctions, including tariffs, on foreign nations in response to declared national emergencies.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs," read the ruling.

Supreme Court Ruling Puts $175 Billion of Tariff Refunds on the Line

In February 2025, Trump imposed tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico for failing to prevent the flow of fentanyl in the U.S. In April, he imposed 10% tariffs on nearly all countries, with higher rates targeting those deemed to engage in unfair trade practices with the U.S.

Since then, the U.S. has collected $175 billion of tariff revenue, according to an estimate from Penn-Wharton Budget Model economists. In other words, $175 billion of tariff refunds could be up for grabs.

However, the ruling noted that the "Court says nothing today about whether, and if so how, the Government should go about returning the billions of dollars that it has collected from importers." This complicates any potential refund process and could require another ruling.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Lucid Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Luxury electric vehicle (EV) maker Lucid Group (LCID) will report its Q4 results on February 24. The stock has lost 8% year-to-date. The company's fourth-quarter deliveries improved, rising more than 70% year-over-year as production of its new Gravity SUV ramped up. However, investors remain cautious ahead of the report due to high costs, strong competition from Tesla (TSLA), and cheaper EV rivals. Also, Lucid still relies on outside funding as it works toward profitability, which continues to weigh on sentiment.

Expectations from Lucid

Wall Street expects a Q4 loss of $2.67 per share, versus a $2.20 loss reported a year ago. Meanwhile, revenues are expected to rise by 96% from the same quarter last year, reaching $459.5 million, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page.

data.title: "Spring Deserves to be Celebrated": Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Notches Up With New Countdown Calendar
data.description:
If you are currently counting the days until spring, you are far, far from alone. In fact, you are joined by no less than home improvement giant Home Depot (HD). Home Depot recently rolled out a limited-edition Spring Countdown Calendar that includes all manner of special product profiles for the time leading up to the first day of spring. This was welcome enough to investors, who gave Home Depot stock a fractional boost in Friday morning's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The part that makes this especially compelling is that it is not merely a calendar, but rather a complete listing of special sale items that gives eager gardeners and home decorators a chance to gear up for spring's arrival. The calendar includes a range of deals on tools, in-season goodies, and more designed to help spark up that feeling that spring is indeed on the way.

It works almost like an advent calendar, reports suggest, with "daily doors" that start on March 1 and open up to a particular product and a QR code to match. The QR code opens up supplementary information about the item in question, including guides detailing how to use the product in question in tackling spring projects. The calendar costs $49.99, but offers a value of $250 off the various products featured therein.

Melting the ICE Protests

However, Home Depot still has a substantial ICE problem, as revealed by a series of customer protests against the store's connection to the Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency. Pittsfield, Massachusetts recently saw one such protest take place, as protesters descended with "ICE Out" signs and various rhythmic chants.

The protesters also turned to ice scraper protests, a growing protest tool in which large numbers of people walk into the stores, purchase a cheap ice scraper, and promptly return it for a full refund. The ice scraper's cost is negligible, but the time it takes to sell it and process a return jams the system up quite effectively.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 16 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.74% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $412.61 per share implies 8.47% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Should Investors Worry? Aptos Plans Big Staking Cut and 10× Gas Hike
data.description:
Layer‑1 blockchain Aptos has proposed a major overhaul of its tokenomics that could reshape incentives for token holders and traders. The foundation's plan, which includes cutting staking rewards, sharply increasing gas fees, and introducing a hard supply cap, is intended to transition the network toward a performance‑driven, potentially deflationary model.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Aptos Cuts Staking Rewards and Raises Gas Fees for Traders

In a Feb. 18 announcement, the Aptos Foundation said it plans to shift from a subsidy‑heavy incentive model to one more closely tied to on‑chain activity. As part of the overhaul, annual staking rewards would drop from about 5.19% to 2.6%, effectively halving new token emissions. For cryptocurrency traders and short-term investors, the changes could pose near-term challenges. 

Lower staking yields could reduce income for Aptos ($APT-USD) holders who lock up tokens, potentially discouraging participation in staking as a rewards strategy. To accelerate deflation, the foundation also proposes a tenfold increase in network gas fees. Because all gas fees are burned, higher transaction costs would directly increase the amount of APT removed from circulation. 

"This, combined with increased on-chain activity from new applications being built on Aptos, would substantially raise the total amount of APT burned and removed from circulation," the Aptos foundation said.

The proposed changes also come ahead of a major unlock milestone in October 2026, when the four-year vesting cycle for early investors and core contributors concludes. Annualized supply unlocks are expected to fall by roughly 60% at that point, while foundation grant distributions are projected to decline more than 50% year-over-year between 2026 and 2027.

New Tokenomics Model to Cap APT Supply

Beyond the staking rewards and reduced gas fees, the most notable aspect of this proposal is a hard supply cap of 2.1 billion APT. With about 1.196 billion already in circulation, of which 1 billion was minted at mainnet launch and 196 million from staking rewards, only about 904 million APT remain available under the cap once the new tokenomics model is implemented. 

Under the revised structure, future issuance would taper gradually as the network approaches that supply cap, with validator compensation increasingly reliant on transaction fees rather than new emissions. The foundation also plans to permanently lock 210 million APT, roughly 18% of the current circulating supply, to reduce the liquid supply and support network stability. 

These tokens would not be sold or redistributed. Instead, operations would be funded through staking rewards generated from the locked allocation, reducing reliance on treasury sales. Aptos is also exploring a programmatic buyback mechanism; an APT reserve funded through cash holdings or ecosystem revenue streams, further tightening supply as the network matures.

What is the Price of Aptos Today?

The price of Aptos is currently sitting around $0.87, with a 24-hour increase of over 2% as of writing. Investors and stakeholders can track Aptos' performance or get updates on similar private crypto companies via TipRanks' private company page.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Twist Bioscience Stock (TWST) Tumbles after Senior Executive Offloads $2M in Shares
data.description:
Shares of Twist Bioscience (TWST) declined about 8% on Friday morning after an insider sale raised fresh attention from investors. The drop followed a regulatory filing showing that Paula Green, Senior Vice President of Human Resources, had sold more than $2 million worth of company stock.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to the SEC filing, Green sold 39,400 Twist Bioscience shares on Tuesday at an average price of $52.40 per share. The sale reduced her stake in the company by 24%, leaving her with roughly 126,000 shares.

Importantly, the move comes after Twist Bioscience stock surged more than 53% so far this year, including a nearly 14% jump on February 2 following the company's FY26 guidance raise.

Should Investors Worry?

While insider sales do not always signal trouble, executives sell stock for many personal or financial reasons.

Twist just delivered a record quarter with $103.7 million in revenue, up 17% year‑over‑year, marking its 12th consecutive quarter of growth. Analysts expect this momentum to continue, forecasting revenue to increase by 16% in the next quarterly report.

The company continues to benefit from growing demand for synthetic DNA across drug discovery and antibody engineering, agriculture and industrial biotech, and data storage research.

Also, Twist Bioscience is investing heavily in scaling its manufacturing platform and expanding its customer base across pharma, academic research, and industrial biotech.

Is TWST a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, TWST stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on six Buys assigned in the last three months. At $52.00, the average Twist Bioscience stock price target implies a 7.19% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: XRP Price Prays for a Catalyst as Garlinghouse Sees 90% Chance for Clarity Act Passing, Says 'Industry Can't Live in Limbo'
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) is currently waiting for a massive decision from Washington that could change its value overnight. Right now, the price is holding steady around $1.42, but the energy behind the scenes is growing. Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse recently shared that he expects a major new law, the Clarity Act, to pass by the end of April. This law would finally give the crypto world the clear rules it has been asking for. If it passes, many analysts believe it will trigger a huge phase of buying that could send the XRP price toward a new highs.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brad Garlinghouse Predicts an End to the Chaos

For a long time, the biggest problem for XRP has been limbo, a state where nobody is quite sure what the rules are. Garlinghouse argues that this confusion has stopped big banks from using XRP to its full potential. During a recent talk on Fox Business, he explained that the industry is tired of waiting for the government to make up its mind. He said: "The industry can't live in limbo."

The passage of the Clarity Act would allow the U.S. to finally decide which agency oversees which coins. Regarding XRP, this shift means it could officially be labeled as a digital commodity like gold (CM:XAUUSD). Such a change would make it much safer for trillion-dollar investment funds to start buying XRP, which would put massive upward pressure on the price.

New Law Creates a Path to $2.00 and Beyond

Traders are already looking at what happens to the XRP price if this law gets signed. Currently, the price is facing some resistance near $1.44, but a victory in Washington would likely blast right through that ceiling.

  • The Short-Term Goal: Many experts see $2.00 as the first major target once the law passes.
  • The Long-Term Dream: Some analysts are even more bullish, suggesting that with the legal clouds gone, XRP could eventually chase its previous highs near $3.84.

When big institutions like banks feel safe holding a coin, they don't just buy a little; they buy millions. This kind of big money entry is exactly what is needed to move the XRP price into a new bracket.

Ripple Prepares for a Massive Surge in Usage

Ripple is so confident that this law will pass that they are changing how they run their business. They have spent nearly $3 billion over the last few years buying other companies to build a massive payment network. Now, they are pausing those purchases to focus on getting everything ready for a surge in new customers. They want to make sure that when the Clarity Act passes, their system can handle a huge increase in people using XRP for daily payments. This integration phase shows that Ripple expects the demand for XRP to explode the moment the law becomes official.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4150.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Trump Tariff Refund Process Is 'Likely to be a Mess,' Warns SCOTUS
data.description:
On Friday, the Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that President Trump overstepped his legal authority by imposing sweeping global tariffs. The decision could put $175 billion of tariff refunds on the table.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The ruling noted that the "Court says nothing today about whether, and if so how, the Government should go about returning the billions of dollars" of tariff revenue. At the same time, SCOTUS cautioned that a potential refund process "is likely to be a 'mess,' as was acknowledged at oral
arguments."

Trump Turns to Backup Plan to Enforce Tariffs

Refunding tariff revenue could require another SCOTUS ruling and will likely be a lengthy process. Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. import tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%.

Trump characterized Friday's ruling as a "disgrace" and reiterated that he has a backup plan to enforce tariffs. That could include Section 301 and Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which allow the president to impose tariffs or trade restrictions to address unfair trade practices or protect domestic industries.

data.title: 'Hold Your Horses,' Says Morgan Stanley as CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Faces a Downgrade
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) shares head into their February 26 earnings report with a lot already priced in. The AI cloud infrastructure provider has been one of the market's biggest beneficiaries of the generative AI buildout, riding demand from large language model developers and hyperscalers scrambling for access to high-performance GPUs. So far this year, the stock has climbed 25%, outperforming large-cap software peers, which have struggled through a choppier stretch.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors have grown accustomed to a steady drumbeat of upbeat updates from the company, particularly through the middle of last year, when deal announcements, new power contracts, capital raises, and M&A activity kept sentiment elevated between earnings reports. This time around, however, the fourth quarter has been comparatively quiet, raising the stakes for the upcoming print.

That is where Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss steps in with a word of caution. Downgrading the stock today from Overweight (i.e., Buy) to Equal-weight (i.e., Neutral), the analyst urges investors to take a breath. After what he calls an "underwhelming Q3" that raised doubts about CoreWeave's execution in a supply-constrained environment, the stock's rally leaves expectations running high into Q4.

Weiss argues this creates "a reasonably difficult setup," one in which management needs to thread the needle on several fronts. Chief among them is power. Active power stood at 590 megawatts at the end of Q3, and the company has guided to exiting fiscal 2025 with more than 850 MW online. According to Weiss, surpassing that 850 MW mark is critical because it "gives credence to the durability of the growth story." Investors will be watching closely for updates on incremental power delivery, progress on resolving prior data center delays, and clarity around the recently discussed goal of contracting an additional 5 gigawatts of power by 2030.

Backlog and bookings are another focal point. In Q3, backlog-based bookings of $6.165 billion fell short of expectations despite a widely discussed $6 billion-plus deal with Nvidia. With fewer large announcements during Q4, Weiss says it becomes harder to triangulate expectations, increasing the importance of evidence that bookings growth extends beyond mega-deals and reflects a broader customer base.

Looking further out, Weiss sees a constructive setup for 2026, with 2.9 GW of contracted capacity expected to come online over the next 12 to 24 months and capital expenditures projected to more than double year over year. Still, the analyst cautions that revenue guidance likely needs to come in above consensus to rebuild confidence, particularly given the company has missed capex targets in consecutive quarters.

Investors reacted swiftly to the more cautious tone, sending CoreWeave shares down 8% as of this writing, with the stock trading around $89. With Weiss maintaining a $99 price target, the upside from here stands at ~12%. (To watch Weiss' track record, click here)

Wall Street, on balance, still sees more upside than downside. CoreWeave holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 recent analyst reviews, with 9 analysts recommending the stock and 8 taking a more neutral stance. The average 12-month price target of $118.57 implies about 33% upside from current levels. (See CRWV stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross
